Details: Chloramines can effectively inactivate S. typhi; however, chloramines are much less efficient than free chlorine, as shown in a summary paper [959] that compares the data of related E. typhosa  inactivation by chlorine [960] and chloramines [961]. A lab study showed that disinfection efficacy is affected by chloramines dose, contact time, pH and temperature [961]. Disinfection activity of chloramine is reduced with increased pH. The study tested two temperature ranges, from 2 to 6 °C and from 20 to 26 °C. In the lower temperature range, the disinfection efficacy of chloramines was lower. The study also showed that contact time and chloramine residual are the primary factors governing disinfection efficacy.
